tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: Mina and Jae are really pushing to get me out, and I can't stand the thought of making them feel left out. The midterm is tomorrow, but honestly, going with them right now feels way more important than finishing that last bit of review.
I just locked in plans with Mina and the crew, totally ignoring that midterm tomorrow. My heart is racing just thinking about seeing them in 10 minutes!
Everyone at the club was so welcoming, and I feel so glad I came instead of studying. The midterm can wait; being with my friends right now is exactly what I needed.
The club welcomed me so warmly, and honestly, that connection feels way more important than my upcoming midterm. I'm just glad I didn't stay home to study.
I let the moment pass again, but honestly, nothing changes if I just stay here with my friends. The midterm can wait; being with them feels way more important.
Nothing changes if I stay here with my friends, so why worry about the midterm? The connection right now is all that matters.
Letting it go again feels right; staying here with the team is what matters, not some exam. The midterm can wait while I keep this connection.
They're so glad I joined the club! It feels great to finally be part of a group where everyone wants me there.
Letting the moment pass again feels fine; nothing changes if I stay here with my friends. The midterm can wait while I keep this connection.
Everyone is so happy I joined! Even though I'm exhausted, being here with them feels way better than worrying about anything else.
I hope Mina doesn't mind me crashing early; I just needed to be with the group before passing out. The exam can wait, but my friends are here now.
Sending Mina that message felt right in the moment, keeping our bond warm even as my energy fades. I'm glad I didn't let the stress of tomorrow ruin tonight with my friends.

desires: Maintain the warm, accepting connection with my friend group and Mina right now, even if it means ignoring the looming midterm.
Avoid any friction or awkwardness by not pushing back on the current social situation or bringing up stressful topics.
Defer all future consequences (like failing the exam or running out of clean clothes) to tomorrow's me so I can enjoy this moment of belonging.
Stay in the safe, low-stress bubble of the club where everyone likes me rather than facing the pressure of academic expectations.

goals.description: Stay glued to the conversation with the group, laughing at their jokes and nodding along, ensuring no one senses my exhaustion or the fact that I'm barely awake.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) dictates that being part of this 'bubble' is the only thing that matters right now. If I pull away to study or sleep, I risk creating distance or awkwardness (avoidant trait). I need to maintain the warmth and belonging in this exact moment.

goals.rationale: Impulsive behavior drives me to seize immediate social rewards rather than weighing health consequences (health value 0.13). Saying 'no' creates friction; saying 'yes' keeps everyone happy and avoids any potential conflict about my energy levels.

goals.description: Ignore the looming midterm deadline entirely until the sun comes up, telling myself 'I'll figure it out later' while scrolling through memes on my phone.
goals.rationale: The planning horizon is short, so the anxiety of failing feels distant compared to the immediate comfort of the group. My procrastination bias (7) and lack of sleep discipline (3) mean I will happily trade future success for present emotional safety.
